name: Razorpay Payment Service — JVM Performance Tuning
scenario: Razorpay's payment service has p99 latency spikes every 30 seconds (traced to G1GC Full GC pauses of 800ms). Also, memory grows 50MB/day (traced to an unbounded static cache). Fix both issues and add comprehensive JVM monitoring.

core.description: JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS (in Dockerfile or env): -XX:+UseG1GC -XX:MaxGCPauseMillis=100 -XX:MaxRAMPercentage=75.0 -XX:+UseContainerSupport -XX:+HeapDumpOnOutOfMemoryError -XX:HeapDumpPath=/tmp/. Comment explaining each flag.
JVMMonitoringService @Service: Gauge for heap utilization (jvm.heap.utilization), Counter for heap warnings (> 80%), @Scheduled check every 30s.
SafePaymentCache @Service: Replace unbounded HashMap with Caffeine cache (maximumSize=1000, expireAfterWrite=10min). Register cache size as Gauge.
@SpringBootTest (3 tests): heap gauge registered + value 0-1, safe cache bounded at 1000, JIT compilation time accessible via CompilationMXBean. All 3 pass.
README: GC log pattern that shows the 800ms pause problem + explanation of fix.

bonus.description: Add ZGC configuration (separate docker-compose profile for latency-critical mode). Explain the G1GC vs ZGC tradeoff.
AllocationRateMonitor: track jvm.gc.memory.allocated rate (MB/s), warn if > 500MB/s. Test: allocation rate is tracked.
WarmUpService @EventListener(ApplicationStartedEvent): makes 50 test calls to payment processing path. Tracks JIT time before and after. Delays readiness probe by 10s after warm-up.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. -XX:MaxRAMPercentage=75.0 replaces -Xmx in containers: heap = 75% of container memory limit (not host RAM). HeapDumpOnOutOfMemoryError is mandatory — without it, OOM has no forensic evidence. G1GC MaxGCPauseMillis=100: target, not guarantee — G1GC will try to keep pauses < 100ms by adjusting collection amount per cycle. Caffeine maximumSize: eviction is asynchronous — allow test assertion margin (≤ maximumSize + 10%). ManagementFactory.getMemoryMXBean().getHeapMemoryUsage() — standard JMX API for heap stats.
